runtime.primaryAgent: Luna coordination with Ascension Brain routing; Hermes planning + Honcho memory for nontrivial work
runtime.hermesGateway: stopped intentionally; OpenClaw owns Telegram, Hermes is called locally for planning/memory through hermes-local.sh ask

models.geminiStatus: google-antigravity auth profile exists in OpenClaw; Hermes Gemini OAuth not logged in

routingNotes.lunaDefault: qwen3.5:cloud for fast smart Telegram chat and coordination; restored because live Telegram usage was good.
routingNotes.hermesDefault: qwen3.5:cloud for fast planning and Honcho memory recall; escalate to deepseek-v4-pro, Claude, or Codex for hard reasoning/coding.
routingNotes.triad: Luna is the Ollama-first coordinator, Star is the Codex coding specialist, Solar is the Claude reasoning/review specialist.
routingNotes.solarAuthRule: Solar must not claim Claude ran unless Claude CLI/auth metadata proves it. If Claude auth is unavailable, disclose degraded fallback mode.
routingNotes.coderNext: qwen3-coder-next:cloud is not in Luna general fallbacks; use only for explicit coding-specialized model requests or delegate coding to Star/Codex
routingNotes.tokenPolicy: preserve Hermes/Honcho/Paperclip/Ouroboros logic; reduce token waste with short RTK briefs and one lightweight orchestration skill instead of broad skill inventory scans
routingNotes.ascensionBrain: Use /Users/ki_mini/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/ascension-brain.mjs to classify nontrivial tasks into Luna, Hermes, Paperclip, Ouroboros, Star/Codex, Solar/Claude, and Gemini routes.
routingNotes.benchmarkNote: Local one-off CLI timing is lower priority than live Telegram behavior; keep qwen3.5:cloud as Luna/Hermes default unless real user-facing regression appears.
routingNotes.solarDefault: sonnet4.6 for normal Solar expert work; opus4.6 only for very hard reasoning/review when explicitly needed

notes.lunaDefault: qwen3.5:cloud for fast smart Telegram chat and coordination; restored because live Telegram usage was good.
notes.hermesDefault: qwen3.5:cloud for fast planning and Honcho memory recall; escalate to deepseek-v4-pro, Claude, or Codex for hard reasoning/coding.
notes.benchmarkNote: Local one-off CLI timing is lower priority than live Telegram behavior; keep qwen3.5:cloud as Luna/Hermes default unless real user-facing regression appears.

honcho.tokenPolicy: tools-mode recall, bounded context, shallow dialectic depth, slower recall cadence, no OpenClaw Telegram injection

memory.note: mem0 is not active; Honcho is active for Hermes only through local self-host and should not be injected directly into OpenClaw Telegram

tokenSaving.policy: short RTK briefs, one lightweight orchestration skill, bounded bootstrap, Paperclip checklist summaries, no broad history dumps

paperclip.policy: durable todo/checklist memory for recovery after session compression, context loss, and agent handoff; do not make it the default Telegram or background memory owner

ouroboros.defaultPolicy: automatic bounded self-check for high-risk, repeat-mistake, release, security, and recurring workflow stabilization; no unbounded background loop

growthAgents.purpose: Paw Studio Apps in Toss launch advertising and SNS operations

shorts.name: ShortsMaker
shorts.role: short-form ad scripts, shot lists, media prompt packs, edit checklists, creative variants

escalation.star: automation code, repo work, upload tooling
escalation.solar: launch strategy, policy risk, reputation risk
escalation.gemini: image/video generation or multimodal inspection when explicitly needed

sns.name: SNSManager
sns.role: platform calendar, captions, hashtag packs, upload checklists, analytics summaries

growthAgents.ouroborosPolicy: explicit-only self-check before repeatable campaign templates or automation rules are treated as stable

star.role: Codex coding, repo implementation, tests, builds, PR preparation

solar.role: Claude architecture, review, strategy, policy, risk reasoning
solar.degradedMode: If claude auth status is not logged in, OpenClaw falls back to Ollama Cloud and Solar must say it is not running true Claude.
solar.statusCheck: claude auth status --json && openclaw channels status --probe

ascensionBrain.purpose: Fast task classifier that preserves Hermes/Honcho/Paperclip/Ouroboros intelligence without re-enabling heavy OpenClaw skill discovery on every Telegram turn.
ascensionBrain.defaultMode: dry-run JSON; execute Hermes only with --execute-hermes or when OpenClaw command tools are explicitly used; Hermes fast path uses minimax-m2.7:cloud

triggers.lunaOnly: routine chat, short coordination, simple answers
triggers.hermesHoncho: multi-step work, durable decisions, ambiguity, memory recall, automation design, repeated problems
triggers.paperclip: todos, checklists, handoffs, campaigns, launch work, context-loss recovery
triggers.ouroboros: high-stakes automation, release/security/billing/public-posting risk, recurring workflows, repeated mistakes
triggers.starCodex: repo changes, coding implementation, tests, PR work, difficult debugging
triggers.solarClaude: architecture, review, policy/legal/risk reasoning, high-level strategy
triggers.gemini: image, video, photo, multimodal generation or inspection
triggers.goal: messages starting with /goal run /Users/ki_mini/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/luna-goal.mjs for bounded continue-until-result loops
triggers.pet: messages starting with /pet run /Users/ki_mini/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/luna-pet.mjs for health/routing smoke checks

guardrails: Do not claim Hermes/Paperclip/Ouroboros ran unless command output confirms it.
Do not use qwen3-coder-next for ordinary chat.
Use short RTK briefs for escalation instead of full session dumps.
Ask before destructive, billing, public posting, account, or irreversible actions.
Goal loops have bounded max turns and pause instead of spending indefinitely.

goal.policy: bounded continue-until-result loop; default max 3 turns; use Paperclip/Hermes/Ouroboros routing for durable work

pet.policy: lightweight health and routing smoke test for OpenClaw, Hermes/Honcho, Paperclip, Ouroboros, Ascension Brain, and goal state
